commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=29b2518bee79fd23c11425a3fc861b10b274122b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `authProviderId` fallback to the shared memory embedding provider resolver and adds a memory-core regression test for resolving `google` to the Gemini adapter.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +30. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: `memorySearch.provider: "google"` reaches `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider("google")`, while the Google adapter is registered as `gemini` with `authProviderId: "google"`; I did not run the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the PR defines an implicit auth-provider alias for memory provider config and the remaining repair depends on that contract decision.

target.title: fix(memory): resolve embedding provider by authProviderId fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- When a user configures `memorySearch.provider` to `"google"`, the embedding provider lookup failed with "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" because the registered adapter id is `"gemini"` while the config-level provider name is `"google"`.
- The gemini memory embedding adapter declares `authProviderId: "google"` (it uses the Google auth provider for API keys).
- Previous approach added duplicate `authProviderId` fallback scans at two call sites.
- **This revision centralizes the fallback in `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider`**, the shared runtime resolver in `src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.ts`, so all consumers agree on the resolution:
  - Memory manager (`memory-core`)
  - Gateway `/v1/embeddings`
  - Startup warnings / plugin-load paths
  - Doctor / status checks
- Removes the duplicated call-site scans from `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` and `src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts`.
- The `listRegisteredMemoryEmbeddingProviders` mock in the regression test is updated to mirror the centralized logic.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` → 8 passed (includes regression test)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` → 36 passed
- Centralized fallback logic verified via simulation of the registered-provider lookup

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix / [ ] Feature / [x] Refactor / [ ] Docs / [ ] Security / [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [x] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [ ] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Configuring `memorySearch.provider: "google"` caused "Unknown memory embedding provider: google" because the gemini adapter id did not match the "google" config-level provider name. The fallback is now centralized so every lookup path agrees.

**Environment tested:** Node 24.x on Linux, vitest + simulated registered-provider lookup matching live gateway state.

**Steps run after the patch:** Simulated the registered-provider state that exists in a live gateway (gemini adapter registered with `authProviderId: "google"`) and verified the centralized `getMemoryEmbeddingProvider` resolves "google" → "gemini".

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
simulatedLookup("gemini"): gemini
simulatedLookup("google"): gemini
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The centralized resolver correctly finds the gemini adapter when queried with "google" via `authProviderId` matching. Direct id lookup ("gemini") continues to work. All existing tests pass with no duplicated fallback code at call sites.

**Not tested:** Live Rocky Linux upgrade flow from 2026.5.26 to 2026.6.1 with real Google AI Studio API key and memory index rebuild.

Closes #90786
target.author: lilan0125

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: LiLan0125
